description_long: This cabin consists of two distinct wooden structures connected by a raised walkway. The main building features a prominent, steeply curved roof with wide overhangs and a series of rectangular windows along its facade, elevated on sturdy stilts. The secondary structure is smaller, with a simpler gable roof and an open porch area with railings and steps leading up. Both buildings are constructed primarily from wood, with visible support beams and slatted details. The main cabin's entrance is accessed via a wooden staircase, and the overall design suggests a blend of traditional and tropical influences. The exterior is finished in warm brown tones, giving the cabin a rustic yet inviting appearance.
description: A two-part wooden cabin with a large curved roof main building and a smaller gabled annex, connected by a raised walkway and featuring porches and railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main cabin's curved roof, central staircase, and the smaller annex with its porch and steps.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the connecting walkway between the two structures and the annex's open porch with railings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main cabin with its window arrangement and the annex's simple gable roof.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the spatial relationship between the main cabin and annex, as well as the elevated walkway and porch.
attribute_roof_type: curved roof on main building with wide overhangs; gable roof on annex; both appear to be wooden shingle or plank construction
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple rectangular windows arranged in a horizontal row on the main building's facade; windows are evenly spaced and of similar size, creating a symmetrical pattern
attribute_door_placement: single main door centered at the front of the main building, accessed by a staircase; secondary door on the annex, opening onto the porch
